name: George Martin
abstract: Sir George Henry Martin (3 January 1926 – 8 March 2016) was an English record producer, arranger, composer, conductor, and musician. He was commonly referred to as the "fifth Beatle" due to his extensive involvement in each of the Beatles' original albums.

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: My heroes were Lieber and Stoller and Spector and then later on George Martin’s work only with The Beatles.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: I saw George years later. He asked me to come and visit his studio in London... He said, 'Oh, absolutely. That was an important occasion for us... I remember that night vividly.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Laurence Juber
about_quotes.evidence: George Martin was one of the first producers I worked with... And I played on an album for Cleo Laine and John Dankworth. And George Martin was producing it
about_quotes.doc_title: Laurence Juber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Glasgow, Scotland, July 27, 2016 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Hey
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer mentions George Martin in the context of Paul McCartney and 'Penny Lane'; Interviewee responds 'Yeah of course' to a general question about studio situations, not specifically confirming a personal interaction with George Martin.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Hey interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 2, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Scott
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer mentions George Martin in the context of Paul McCartney's 'Penny Lane' trumpet solo; Interviewee responds 'Right.' without adding personal interaction details.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Scott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jason Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: I’d ask Delfeayo about it, and he’s like, “Oh yeah, man, Beatles have some stuff. Plus, from a production standpoint, George Martin” was doing this and that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jason Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
